title: Augmentation techniques for video surveillance in the visible and thermal spectral range
abstract: arXiv:2606.13042v2 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: In intelligent video surveillance, cameras record image sequences during day and night. Commonly, this demands different sensors. To achieve a better performance it is not unusual to combine them. We focus on the case that a long-wave infrared camera records continuously and in addition to this, another camera records in the visible spectral range during daytime and an intelligent algorithm supervises the picked up imagery. More accurate, our task is multispectral CNN-based object detection. At first glance, images originating from the visible spectral range differ between thermal infrared ones in the presence of color and distinct texture information on the one hand and in not containing information about thermal radiation that emits from objects on the other hand. Although color can provide valuable information for classification tasks, effects such as varying illumination and specialties of different sensors still represent signif

To achieve a better performance it is not unusual to combine them. We focus on the case that a long-wave infrared camera records continuously and in addition to this, another camera records in the visible spectral range during daytime and an intelligent algorithm supervises the picked up imagery. More accurate, our task is multispectral CNN-based object detection. translated_title: 可见光与热红外光谱范围内视频监控的增强技术
translated_abstract: 在智能视频监控中，摄像头在白天和夜晚记录图像序列。通常，这需要不同的传感器。为了获得更好的性能，结合使用这些传感器并不罕见。我们重点关注这样一个场景：一台长波红外摄像头持续记录，同时另一台摄像头在白天记录可见光谱范围内的图像，并由智能算法对采集到的图像进行监控。更准确地说，我们的任务是基于多光谱CNN的目标检测。乍一看，可见光谱范围内的图像与热红外图像的区别在于，前者具有颜色和清晰的纹理信息，而后者不包含物体发出的热辐射信息。尽管颜色可以为分类任务提供有价值的信息，但诸如光照变化和不同传感器的特性等因素仍然构成显著挑战。
tldr: 本文提出了一种针对可见光与热红外视频监控的多光谱CNN目标检测增强技术，通过结合不同光谱传感器的优势来应对光照变化和传感器差异带来的挑战。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes an augmentation technique for multispectral CNN-based object detection in video surveillance, combining visible and thermal infrared sensors to address challenges from varying illumination and sensor differences.
